response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack engineer with 15+ years across web, mobile, and some embedded work, but is a poor fit for this role. The position has hard requirements that are absent or weak: native-level Japanese proficiency is not listed at all (languages are English, Romanian, German basic), strong C-for-UNIX experience is minimal and dated (a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments years ago and a C++ thesis), and there is no evidence of Linux kernel source-modification, embedded LAN-switch firmware, or network security technologies such as MACsec, 802.1X, or L2/L3 switching. Core qualifications for this specific embedded security role are not met.
strengths: Long, broad software career with strong testing/TDD and DevOps discipline
Some C/C++ exposure (National Instruments IDE, C++ thesis) and embedded-adjacent work
Comfortable reading/communicating in English; self-driven and collaborative
concerns: No native-level Japanese proficiency, a hard requirement
C/UNIX skills are weak and outdated; primary stack is JS/TS and DLang
No Linux kernel modification, switch firmware, or network security (MACsec/802.1X) experience
